title: Get notification when an automation fails
excerpt: I experienced several times that Monday was not doing what it was supposed to do. Later, I discovered that the automations did not work because of a timeout. It would be nice to get a notification when an automation fail…

posts.author: FdL (Franck de Luca)
posts.cookedHtml:
I experienced several times that Monday was not doing what it was supposed to do. Later, I discovered that the automations did not work because of a timeout. It would be nice to get a notification when an automation fails, so that we can correct or relaunch the automation quickly instead of discovering the issue by chance.
Thanks

posts.author: mw01 (MW)
posts.cookedHtml: Is anyone else getting random notifications saying automations have failed but when you go to your automations there is nothing that shows why it failed?

posts.author: Michael-PT (Michael Albers)
posts.cookedHtml: Yup, me. That’s why I am searching the board now. Could it be a deleted automation that somehow generates the notification? When checking ‘automation activity’ no failure is mentioned.

posts.author: mw01 (MW)
posts.cookedHtml: I’d like to get an answer on this. Searched the board as well.

posts.author: LaunchMe (Shawna Culp)
posts.cookedHtml: I need to be notified when an automation fails so that if it is a part of a larger workflow I understand the consequences and can troubleshoot immediately. I currently get an automation when one is deactivated. I would just like one when they fail as well.

posts.author: Fan8 (Fannie Huet)
posts.cookedHtml: There is still no notification when an automation stops working. Could Monday do something about this and set up an email notification to the creator of the automation to advise that it stopped working?
